project_info.name: Omid (Object-MIDI)
project_info.standard: Global Unified Audio, Control, & Driver Standard
project_info.philosophy: Predictable, low-overhead, zero-copy, highly parallelized, bare-metal friendly (#![no_std] core) digital music instrument protocol.

directory_layout.file: Crate manifest configuring standard and no_std features, cdylib target, bridge feature, and debian package packaging metadata.
Build script automating C header generation in include/omid.h via cbindgen.
MIT Open Source License terms.
Apache 2.0 Open Source License terms.
Library root conditionally importing standard modules and exporting core APIs, unit tests, and FFI layer.
Core enums mapping EventTypes and ForceProfiles alongside OmidFlags bitwise helpers.
OmidPacket implementation detailing serialization, haptic feedback, and 12-bit/16-bit high-resolution ADC constructors and getters.
High-performance, lock-free Single-Producer Single-Consumer (SPSC) Ring Buffer with 64-byte cache line alignment and power-of-two capacity masking.
TopologyDescriptor defining physical hardware coordinates and resolution mapping.
Unified Audio & Control Transport (UACT) frame modeling, zero-heap circular buffer Demuxer, and sample-accurate ClockSynchronizer.
SPMC OmidHostDispatcher executing real-time thread affinity pinning, Voice ID dispatching, registered DSP callbacks, and Instant-based latency hooks.
OmidDriver trait, MockHardwareDriver endpoints (ep1_in / ep1_out), platform drivers (Linux io_uring / Windows IOCP), and IoT drivers (Bluetooth 5 and WiFi sockets).
FFI module exporting C-compatible wrapper functions for OmidPacket, OmidFlags, and TopologyDescriptor.
MIDI 1.0 (Note, CC, Pitch Bend) and MIDI 2.0 Sysex8 UMP translation utilities.
On-the-fly legacy bridge daemon using midir to convert standard MIDI port data to/from Omid.
Contains native wrappers and FFI loaders for C++, Go, Python, TypeScript, Dart, C#, Java, and Kotlin.
JavaScript WebUSB bindings allowing browser-based DAWs to parse and stream Omid packets directly.
ConfigFS script exposing Raspberry Pi/BeagleBone USB controllers as Omid hardware gadgets.
WinUSB Windows driver installation template.
Embedded guide outlining C11 packet layer definitions, FreeRTOS/Zephyr task loops, and I2S interleaving designs.

byte_map.field: payload (Zero-copy IEEE 754 f32, dual u16 XY coords, or raw ADC bits)

adc_mapping.description: When the RawData flag (bit 1 of flags) is set, the payload represents a raw integer value.
adc_mapping.adc12: Capped at 4095, stored in the lower 12 bits of the payload.
adc_mapping.adc16: Stored in the lower 16 bits of the payload.

haptic_mapping.payload_byte: f32 representing actuator electromagnetic resistance intensity

uact_frame.description: Interleaves synchronous multi-channel digital audio and control packets under a single physical clock domain (e.g. 122.88 MHz PLL clock).
uact_frame.formula: Frame Size = C * 4 + 8 bytes (where C is the audio channel count)

clock_synchronization.formula: Sample Offset = (Timer Delta / PLL Clock Frequency) * Sample Rate

spsc_queue.lock_free: Yes (UnsafeCell and AtomicUsize index wrapping with Acquire/Release memory ordering)
spsc_queue.allocation: Zero heap allocation (buffer array of Option<T> on stack/static memory)
spsc_queue.cache_alignment: 64-byte aligned indexes to prevent false sharing cache bouncing
spsc_queue.index_optimization: Mask-based bitwise index wrapping `& (N - 1)` (requires N to be power-of-two)

topology.receiver_thread: Core 1 (Isolated) - drains raw USB bulk streams and routes packets.
topology.dispatcher_logic: Voice ID = Object ID % Worker Count. Routes packets to SpscRingBuffer queues without mutexes.
topology.worker_threads: Worker pools pinned to separate CPU cores (Core 2, 3, etc.) running adaptive spin-looping.
topology.pinning: On Linux, directly pins threads via raw pthread FFI pthread_setaffinity_np.

platform_drivers.linux: LinuxDriver opening `/dev/omid0` file handles read/written asynchronously via io_uring submit/completion queues.
platform_drivers.windows: WindowsDriver exposing WinUSB Direct DMA via overlapped IOCP threads.
platform_drivers.macos: MacosDriver utilizing Darwin Thread Time Constraint real-time policies.

iot_drivers.bluetooth5: BleDriver advertising MTU negotiations (23 to 512 bytes), BLE 2M PHY, and L2CAP Connection-Oriented Channels (CoC).
iot_drivers.wifi: WifiDriver supporting live TCP/UDP socket connections supporting socket-level configuration options like TCP_NODELAY.

compilation_and_verification.latency_metrics: Control pipeline latency targeted at under 10 microseconds queue-to-queue transfer.
